skill.name: humanize
skill.description: Rewrite AI-generated text through the HumanizerAI API with selectable rewrite intensity and credit reporting.
skill.summary: Humanizes submitted text through HumanizerAI and reports rewrite scores and credit usage.
skill.author: humanizerai

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and example code fences in SKILL.md. The skill does make a real external API request, uses an environment API key, and explicitly advertises bypassing AI detectors including Turnitin, so publication should be withheld pending policy and security review.

high_findings.title: AI Detection Evasion Intent
high_findings.description: The skill description says it transforms AI-generated text to bypass AI detectors like GPTZero, Turnitin, and Originality.ai. This creates academic and professional integrity risk even though it is not direct code malware.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evasion purpose is explicit in the skill metadata and the intensity table names aggressive mode as bypass mode. This is direct semantic evidence, not only a static pattern.

high_findings.title: External API Receives User Text and API Token
high_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to send user-provided text to https://humanizerai.com/api/v1/humanize with a bearer token from HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY. Users may submit confidential text, and the skill does not warn about third-party data sharing.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint, payload, and authorization header are all documented in the skill. The privacy risk is clear, although the API call is part of the declared feature.

medium_findings.title: Environment API Key Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill requires HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY and instructs troubleshooting around that environment variable. This is a legitimate integration pattern but increases exposure if prompts or logs reveal configuration details.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable and bearer token usage are explicit. Risk is moderate because the key is used for authentication to the stated service, not shown being exfiltrated elsewhere.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged multiple Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell command execution. No executable script or instruction to run local shell commands was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown formatting, example command text, or sample output. I did not find evidence of actual command execution in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Dashboard and Website Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded dashboard and homepage URLs are support links for credits and API keys. They are still external destinations and should be disclosed to users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links are plainly visible and match the service domain used by the skill. I found no evidence that these support links perform hidden data transfer.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 32 appear to be false positives. No cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation is present on those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a prose description and line 32 is a Markdown table header. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Network Request With User Content
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs a POST request to an external service using the user text as request body. This is the core capability, but it creates privacy and data handling risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented API call includes the endpoint and payload containing user text. The behavior is explicit in the skill instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token From Environment Variable
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY as a bearer token for the external request. Secrets must not be shown in responses or sent to any unrelated endpoint.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The authorization header and environment variable name are explicit. This is a standard integration pattern with real secret-handling requirements.

content.value_statement: AI-drafted text can sound repetitive or mechanical. This skill sends selected text to HumanizerAI and returns a rewritten version with score and credit details.
actual_capabilities: Parses text supplied to the humanize command.
Accepts light, medium, or aggressive rewrite intensity.
Sends text to the HumanizerAI humanize API endpoint.
Returns humanized text with before and after scores.
Reports processed word count and remaining credits.
Provides basic guidance for credit, API key, and rate limit errors.
limitations: Requires a valid HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends submitted text to a third-party service.
Uses credits based on processed word count.
Should not be used to misrepresent authorship or evade required disclosure.

use_cases.title: Revise Draft Tone
use_cases.description: Rewrite a short AI-assisted draft so it sounds less repetitive and easier to read.

use_cases.title: Prepare Client Copy
use_cases.description: Convert a rough generated paragraph into a more natural version before human review.

use_cases.title: Compare Rewrite Intensity
use_cases.description: Test light, medium, and aggressive rewriting on the same text to choose an editing level.

prompt_templates.title: Humanize a Paragraph
prompt_templates.prompt: /humanize Please rewrite this paragraph in a natural style: [paste paragraph]

prompt_templates.title: Use Light Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: /humanize --intensity light Keep the meaning and structure close to the original: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Use Balanced Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: /humanize --intensity medium Rewrite this draft for clear, natural business communication: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Compare Strong Rewrite
prompt_templates.prompt: /humanize --intensity aggressive Rewrite this text more substantially, then explain what changed: [paste text]

output_examples.input: A short product update sounds repetitive and formal.
output_examples.output: The response provides a smoother version, shows score improvement, lists words processed, and reports remaining credits.

output_examples.input: A user asks for light editing on a polished paragraph.
output_examples.output: The response keeps the main phrasing, adjusts awkward sentences, and notes that the rewrite used light intensity.

output_examples.input: A user has insufficient HumanizerAI credits.
output_examples.output: The response explains how many credits are needed, how many are available, and points to the HumanizerAI dashboard.

best_practices: Review rewritten text before publishing or submitting it.
Do not submit confidential text unless third-party processing is approved.
Choose the lowest rewrite intensity that solves the writing problem.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to hide prohibited AI assistance.
Do not submit private, regulated, or client-sensitive text without approval.
Do not publish rewrites without checking accuracy and tone.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It sends supplied text to HumanizerAI and returns a more natural rewritten version.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY to be available in the environment.

faq.question: Does it send my text outside the tool?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill sends the submitted text to the HumanizerAI API.

faq.question: Can I choose rewrite strength?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports light, medium, and aggressive intensity options.

faq.question: Does it use credits?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill states that one processed word uses one credit.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential content?
faq.answer: Use caution. Do not send confidential content unless your organization approves HumanizerAI processing.
